Chadian nationals are always exempt from visa in their own country. The evisa.td portal exemption list is aimed at foreign nationals; Chadian nationals entry is implicitly guaranteed. Listed here for completeness.

Wikipedia had noted Togo was removed from the portal list at the December 2024 e-visa launch, but the live evisa.td articles/3 page accessed June 2026 confirms Togo is present on the exempt list. Duration not specified on official portal.

e_visa.nationality: All nationalities not on the visa-free exempt list
e_visa.notes: Chad's e-Visa system via evisa.td is available to all foreigners not exempt from visa requirements. From 11 May 2026, all visa applications must be submitted exclusively online via evisa.td; visas issued outside the platform after 21 May 2026 are invalid at the border. Processing times: standard 10 business days, express 72 hours. Applications must be submitted at least 7 days before travel and no more than 60 days in advance (per the note circulaire). Extension is permitted only once for 15- and 30-day visas. Visa fees set by Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026. Diplomatic and courtesy visas remain handled by Chadian embassies and missions abroad. EXCEPTION: As of June 5, 2025, Chad has suspended visa issuance to US citizens (reciprocal response to the US travel ban on Chadian nationals). The US fully suspended visa issuance to Chadian nationals effective January 1, 2026 (Proclamation 10998). As of June 2026, no official announcement of lifting either suspension has been made. A 180-day review period for the US ban was expected around mid-to-late June 2026, but no lifting has been confirmed. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required for entry. All visitors must register with police within 72 hours of arrival.

visa_policy.notes: Chad's official e-visa platform (evisa.td) lists 24 foreign nationalities exempt from visa requirements (the portal also lists Tchad/Chad itself, making 25 total entries). All other foreigners must apply for an e-Visa online. As of 11 May 2026, all new visa applications must be submitted exclusively via evisa.td; manual entry authorizations have been abolished. Legal basis: Décret n°2299/PR/PM/MSPI/2025 and Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026. Note: Chad suspended visa issuance to US citizens effective June 5, 2025 (reciprocal measure in response to the US travel ban on Chadian nationals). The US fully suspended visas to Chadian nationals effective January 1, 2026 (Presidential Proclamation 10998). As of June 2026, neither suspension has been lifted per available information. The 'Congo' entry on the evisa.td exempt list refers to the Republic of the Congo (COG/Brazzaville); the Democratic Republic of Congo (COD/Kinshasa) is NOT on the list. NOTE: Burkina Faso and Philippines were on the previous record but are NOT confirmed on the live evisa.td exempt list as of June 2026 - both have been removed from visa_free and moved to data_quality.gaps pending re-confirmation from the official portal.

visa_types.name: e-Visa Touristique (Tourist e-Visa) - Standard
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for tourism, sightseeing, and leisure visits to Chad. Applied for online via the official evisa.td platform.
visa_types.notes: The note-circulaire (evisa.td/articles/11) explicitly references 15-day and 30-day visa durations and states 'extension of visa is authorised only once for visas of 15 and 30 days.' The 30-day option is the standard tourist stay. Exact fee not published in publicly-accessible pages of evisa.td; set by Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026. Must apply at least 7 days before travel and no more than 60 days in advance. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required at immigration. All visitors must register with police within 72 hours of arrival. Processing may be extended during high-volume periods.

visa_types.name: e-Visa Touristique (Tourist e-Visa) - Express
visa_types.purpose: Fast-track short-stay visa for tourism, sightseeing, and leisure visits to Chad with 72-hour processing.
visa_types.notes: Express processing track confirmed in evisa.td/articles/11 (note-circulaire): '72 heures' turnaround. Same conditions as standard tourist e-Visa. Fee set by Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 - exact amount not published in publicly-accessible portal pages. Applications must be submitted at least 7 days before travel.

visa_types.name: e-Visa d'Affaires (Business e-Visa) - Standard
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for business meetings, conferences, commercial activities, and professional visits to Chad.
visa_types.notes: The evisa.td platform explicitly states it 'covers all visa categories' (all visa categories per the new tariff grid). Business visas require invitation letter from a Chadian company/organisation, and depending on the inviting entity type (private individual, private company, public institution, international organisation) different supporting documents are required per the note-circulaire. Notarised and legalised invitation is required for private invitees. Exact fee set by Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 - not published publicly. Must apply 7-60 days in advance.

visa_types.name: e-Visa d'Affaires (Business e-Visa) - Express
visa_types.purpose: Fast-track business visa for commercial visits to Chad with 72-hour processing.
visa_types.notes: Express track (72 hours) confirmed in the note-circulaire. Same documentation requirements as standard business e-Visa. Fee amount not published publicly.

visa_types.name: e-Visa de Transit (Transit e-Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa permitting transit through Chad en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Transit is one of the standard visa purposes processed through evisa.td. The note-circulaire references 15-day visas as one of the two standard durations (alongside 30-day). Transit visas most commonly correspond to the 15-day stay. Exact fee set by Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 - not publicly disclosed. Note: aggregators mention a 48-hour airside transit exemption but this is NOT confirmed on any official Chad government page and is not recorded here.

visa_types.name: Visa Diplomatique / de Courtoisie (Diplomatic/Courtesy Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Visa issued to holders of diplomatic passports and courtesy visa recipients; remains under the exclusive jurisdiction of the Chadian Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
visa_types.notes: Explicitly excluded from the evisa.td online platform per the article on new tariffs: 'Les visas diplomatiques et de courtoisie relèvent de la compétence exclusive du Ministère des Affaires Étrangères' (Diplomatic and courtesy visas fall under the exclusive jurisdiction of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs). The Chad Embassy Paris 2019 document (ambatchadparis.fr) states visa is free ('gratuit') for holders of Diplomatic or Service passports. Additionally, Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017 grants visa-free entry (no visa required) to holders of diplomatic passports from 20 specific countries. These are processed through Chadian embassies and missions abroad, not through evisa.td.

visa_types.name: Visa de Longue Durée / Visa de Résidence (Long-Stay / Residence Visa)
visa_types.purpose: Visa for foreigners intending to reside in Chad for extended periods, including for work, study, family reunification, or other long-term purposes.
visa_types.notes: The evisa.td platform states it covers 'all visa categories' under the new tariff decree. Long-stay and residence permits are distinct from short-stay tourist/business e-visas and would fall under separate categories within the same platform. The note-circulaire references 'work contracts' and 'employment documentation' as supporting documents, indicating work/long-stay visa categories exist within the system. Specific fee, validity, and duration details for long-stay visas are not published in the publicly-accessible pages of evisa.td. The Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 governs all fees.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic passports issued to nationals of these 20 countries can visit Chad without a visa for up to 90 days. Source: Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017, dated 8 August 2017, issued by Chad's Ministry of Security and Public Immigration (MSPI). Note: the Chad Embassy in Paris document (2019) confirms diplomatic AND service passport holders pay no visa fee ('Le visa est gratuit pour les détenteurs de Passeports Diplomatiques ou de Services'), but that document describes a fee waiver for a required visa, not a visa-free waiver - it is therefore NOT a separate service-passport visa-free rule. The visa-free waiver in Circulaire 002 names only diplomatic passports. The 20-country list is confirmed by the Wikipedia Visa policy of Chad article citing the same circular.

cbi.notes: No Citizenship-by-Investment program found for Chad on any official government source. No official CBI program exists as of June 2026.

agreements: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) - free movement of persons among member states (Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Republic of Congo, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon)

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 re-validation: evisa.td/articles/3 re-fetched and confirmed same 24-country visa-free exempt list (Barbade, Bénin, Bermudes, Cameroun, Congo, Côte d'Ivoire, Dominique, Gabon, Gambie, Guinée-Équatoriale, Haïti, Île Maurice, Îles Cook, Malaisie, Mali, Micronésie, Niger, Nigéria, République centrafricaine, Rwanda, Saint-Vincent-et-les-Grenadines, Singapour, Surinam, Togo). No additions or removals vs. previous record. evisa.td homepage and articles/8 tariff page also re-fetched and consistent. note-circulaire re-confirmed (10 business day standard; 72-hour express; 7–60 day application window; extension once only for 15- and 30-day visas). No policy changes found.

2026-06-29: US/Chad reciprocal visa suspension update. A US District Court (Rhode Island) on June 5, 2026, vacated USCIS adjudication-hold policies PM-602-0192 and PM-602-0194 (USCIS confirmed pausing implementation June 12, 2026). However, these policies addressed USCIS benefit application processing - NOT Proclamation 10998's travel and visa ban itself. Proclamation 10998 (effective January 1, 2026, fully suspending US visa issuance to Chadian nationals) remains in force as of June 29, 2026; no Presidential proclamation lifting or modifying Proclamation 10998 for Chad has been found. Chad's reciprocal ban on US citizens (effective June 5, 2025) likewise shows no official lifting announcement. Notes updated to reflect this distinction. US Embassy Chad (td.usembassy.gov) URL returns technical difficulties; page content unreadable - gap preserved.

2026-06-27 re-validation: evisa.td portal (articles/3 page) re-fetched and confirmed 24-country foreign-national visa-free exempt list. This differs from the previous record of 27 entries (including Chad itself).

2026-06-27: REMOVED Burkina Faso from visa_free. The live evisa.td articles/3 page shows only 24 countries and Burkina Faso is NOT among them. Wikipedia also explicitly states Burkina Faso 'was not included in the visa exemption list on the Chad eVisa portal' when it launched December 11, 2024. Per safety rules (false visa-free = worst error), Burkina Faso has been removed and moved to data_quality.gaps.

2026-06-27: REMOVED Philippines from visa_free. The live evisa.td articles/3 page shows only 24 countries and Philippines is NOT among them. Previous record included Philippines with a note citing evisa.td, but the re-fetched portal content does not confirm Philippines. Moved to data_quality.gaps pending re-confirmation.

2026-06-27: Confirmed Equatorial Guinea (Guinée-Équatoriale) IS present on the live evisa.td articles/3 exempt list. Wikipedia had noted it was removed at December 2024 launch, but the live portal as of June 2026 shows it present. Entry retained with explanatory note.

2026-06-27: Confirmed Togo IS present on the live evisa.td articles/3 exempt list. Wikipedia had noted it was removed at December 2024 launch, but the live portal as of June 2026 shows it present. Entry retained with explanatory note.
2026-06-27: Updated source_url for all visa_free entries from evisa.td/ to evisa.td/articles/3/blog-details.html, which is the page that explicitly renders the 'Pays exemptés' list.

2026-06-27: US citizen visa suspension status updated. Confirmed per US State Department sources: US suspended visas to Chadian nationals under Proclamation 10998 (effective January 1, 2026). Chad's reciprocal ban on US citizens from June 5, 2025 remains in effect as of June 2026. A 180-day review was expected mid-to-late June 2026, but no lifting has been confirmed. Notes updated accordingly.
2026-06-27: policy notes field updated to reflect 24-country portal list (not 27 as previously stated).

2026-06-27: max_stay_days for visa-free entries: official portal does not publish a duration. Secondary/aggregator sources suggest 90 days; this cannot be confirmed from primary source and remains null.
Diplomatic passport conditional access list (Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017, 20 countries, 90 days) confirmed unchanged.
No new CBI, RBI, or fast-track programs found on any official source.
CEMAC free-movement agreement entry confirmed accurate.
Standard processing: 10 business days; Express processing: 72 hours - both confirmed in note-circulaire.

Prior validation notes (June 2026): evisa.td portal re-fetched and confirmed 27-country visa-free list exactly matches prior record - this is now superseded by 2026-06-27 re-validation which found only 24 countries on the live portal.
gaps: Burkina Faso visa-free status: was in previous record but NOT found on live evisa.td articles/3 exempt list as of June 2026; Wikipedia confirms it was removed at December 2024 e-visa launch. Requires re-confirmation directly from the official evisa.td portal (a dynamic list on the homepage may render differently from articles/3). REMOVED from visa_free pending official confirmation.

Philippines visa-free status: was in previous record but NOT found on live evisa.td articles/3 exempt list as of June 2026. Requires re-confirmation directly from the official evisa.td portal. REMOVED from visa_free pending official confirmation.
max_stay_days for visa-free nationalities not specified on the official evisa.td page; exact duration requires checking specific bilateral agreements or regulations not published on the portal
Exact e-visa fees/tariffs: Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 is referenced but not published in full text on evisa.td
No official Chad government website (gouvernement.td, mae.td) was accessible with detailed visa policy pages beyond evisa.td
No official digital-nomad, skilled-worker, or investor-residence programs found on official sources
RBI/fast-track programs: no official Chad program found; omitted rather than recorded from non-official sources

US citizen visa suspension current status: Chad government has not published an official lifting notice; status as of June 29, 2026 inferred from absence of any official announcement. A US court (Rhode Island, June 5, 2026) vacated USCIS adjudication-hold policies but did NOT lift Proclamation 10998 itself. Proclamation 10998 travel/visa ban on Chadian nationals remains in force; Chad's reciprocal ban on US citizens (effective June 5, 2025) likewise remains in force as of June 29, 2026.

Conditional access - third-country visa/residence permit holders: aggregators mention no-visa rule for holders of valid US/Canada/UK/Schengen visa - NO official Chad government source (evisa.td, mae.td, MSPI) confirms this rule; NOT recorded per official-source-first policy
Conditional access - service passport holders: fee waiver confirmed for service passport holders by Embassy Paris document, but no official visa-free waiver for service passports found; not recorded

Diplomatic passport waiver - Circulaire 002/MSPI/SG/2017: original document URL is now 404; only an archived version (web.archive.org) remains accessible; the 20-country list is cited from this archived source
Transit visa policy: Wikipedia mentions 48-hour transit exemption; no confirmation on official evisa.td portal; not recorded
ID-card entry: Wikipedia mentions Cameroon, CAR, Republic of Congo, and Gabon nationals may enter using national ID cards instead of passports; not confirmed on official evisa.td portal; not recorded

Specific fee amounts for each visa type: Arrêté conjoint n°033/PR/PM/MFBEPCI/MSPI/2026 governs all fees but the full text is not publicly accessible via evisa.td; the 2019 Embassy Paris PDF is image-based and could not be machine-read

Work visa, student visa, family visa categories: evisa.td states it covers 'all visa categories' but specific category names and requirements beyond tourist/business/transit are not enumerated in publicly accessible pages
Validity period (from date of issue until it must be used): not published on evisa.td for any visa type

Equatorial Guinea and Togo status: Wikipedia states both were removed from the evisa.td portal at December 2024 e-visa launch, but the live portal (articles/3) as of June 2026 shows both present. The conflict between Wikipedia and the live portal is unresolved; entries retained with notes but flagged for re-verification.

outbound_official.note: no official Chad government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Chad's MFA website (diplomatie.gouv.td) is in maintenance mode and not publicly accessible as of June 2026. The Ministry of Public Security and Immigration (securite.gouv.td) does not publish an outbound visa-free list. No other official Chad government domain (.gouv.td, .td) was found to host a list of countries where Chadian ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa.
